title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Harkness and Colin discuss a girl who discovered a body and expressed extreme terror. Harkness explains the peculiar layout of Wilbraham Crescent, noting that the street is built back-to-back, requiring a detour through Albany Road to reach the second half. Colin is searching for number 61, which Harkness identifies as the home of an architect named Brand. Harkness describes Brand as a dishonest architect who uses inferior materials to commit commercial fraud. He notes that Brand recently escaped bankruptcy after his Canadian wife inherited a large sum of money following the deaths of her grandfather and her father in an air disaster. Harkness mentions that the domestic tax department previously investigated Brand's sudden wealth but found nothing.

Harkness and Colin then travel to the Cavendish Agency on Wangfujing Street. After passing a photography studio and coal merchants, they enter the agency and are led by a receptionist to the office of Miss Martin-Dell, a woman in her fifties with reddish-brown hair. While Harkness questions her, Colin observes her collection of autographed photos from various mystery and romance novelists. Harkness asks about Sheila Webb, an employee who is currently out on business. Miss Martin-Dell states that Webb has been with the agency for a year and may be at the Heron Hotel for a five o'clock appointment. She adds that Webb previously worked at a firm in London, possibly a property firm, and has good references stored in her safe.
